objective: Recover the forecast quality of the Round 44 per-symbol shared causal distributional TCN while raising independently initialized median-forecast agreement above the unchanged 0.500 floor through time-series-specific dynamic error bounds or explicit peer consistency.

diagnosis: Round 44's per-symbol shared TCN beat the unconditional pinball baseline and had positive pooled rank association at all four horizons, but its minimum pairwise seed correlation was 0.452.
Round 44 reached the best early-stop loss at epoch 3 or 4 while training loss continued to fall, which is direct evidence of rapid noise fitting.
Round 45 joint training preserved weak forecast skill but reduced minimum seed agreement to 0.189 for AdamW and 0.181 for SAM.
SAM marginally improved early-stop pinball loss but worsened seed stability and therefore failed its frozen optimizer-ablation gate.
Round 45's seed-consensus action mapping restored activity with 898 to 947 trades, but neither candidate passed forecast or economic gates and drawdown reached 29.48 to 44.10 percent at base cost.

research_basis.finding_used: WaveBound uses a slow EMA target network to set dynamic, output-specific lower bounds on training error and was reported to reduce unstable time-series overfit across multiple architectures, including TCNs.
research_basis.limitation: The published experiments use deterministic MSE forecasting on non-crypto benchmarks; the pinball-loss adaptation is an explicit new ablation and carries no assumed benefit.

research_basis.finding_used: The official implementation evaluates the EMA target model, uses 0.99 decay, starts dynamic bounding after a warm-up, and uses a small positive epsilon in the bound.
research_basis.limitation: The upstream implementation targets older PyTorch and CUDA workflows; this repository independently implements only the documented loss and EMA update on DirectML.

research_basis.finding_used: Deep mutual learning shows that independently initialized peer networks can improve by learning collaboratively without a pre-trained teacher.
research_basis.limitation: The paper concerns classification; Round 46 uses a separately defined standardized-median regression consistency objective and does not assume its reported accuracy transfers.

research_basis.finding_used: Output-consistency regularization can reduce stochastic submodel disagreement while retaining the supervised objective.
research_basis.limitation: R-Drop uses bidirectional KL divergence for probabilistic classification and language tasks, not quantile return regression.

research_basis.finding_used: Constrained probabilistic forecast combination supports retaining independently valid quantile forecasts rather than collapsing uncertainty into one point estimate.
research_basis.limitation: Load forecasting is not financial trading evidence; Round 46 keeps a simple elementwise median ensemble and does not fit post-outcome ensemble weights.

research_basis.finding_used: Algorithmic stability for temporally dependent, non-i.i.d. processes is a relevant generalization criterion, so seed agreement remains a hard gate rather than a cosmetic metric.
research_basis.limitation: The theoretical bounds do not validate this neural architecture or establish financial edge.

data_contract.target_and_role_contract: Bit-for-bit identical to the hash-bound Round 44 and Round 45 compounded hourly return, completeness, and role-boundary contract.
data_contract.cache_contract: Read the five hash-verified external Round 45 NumPy cache files only after reproducing their aggregate dataset identity; no cache value can override source-certificate lineage.

chronological_roles.purpose: Mechanism development only; no role can support promotion or a live-trading claim.

normalization_contract.feature_and_target_scalers: Bit-for-bit Round 44 mean, population-standard-deviation, clip, median, and scaled-MAD definitions.

candidates.mechanism: WaveBound dynamic pinball-error lower bounds with an EMA target model
Three independently initialized peers trained on common batches with supervised pinball plus standardized-median agreement
candidates.consistency_definition: For each horizon and peer pair, one half of the mean squared difference between centered, unit-population-standard-deviation median forecasts over the batch and supervised time axis; algebraically this is 1 minus Pearson correlation before numerical epsilon.
candidates.early_stop_model: all three peers at the common epoch with the best ensemble-median early-stop pinball

model_contract.input_layout: One symbol sequence per sample with the same 71 target and causal cross-asset context features used by Round 44.
model_contract.output_layout: Four horizons times five monotone quantiles for the sampled symbol.
model_contract.quantile_parameterization: Per horizon, predict a median and four nonnegative softplus gaps so q10 <= q25 <= q50 <= q75 <= q90.
model_contract.supervised_loss: Equal-weight normalized pinball loss across four horizons and five quantiles.
model_contract.ensemble: Elementwise median of three independently initialized forecasts; no fitted ensemble weights.
model_contract.candidate_selection: None; both pre-registered mechanisms complete the same consumed-development evaluation.
model_contract.compute_backend: DirectML required for this host run; CPU remains a separately declared portability mode.

mechanism_gate.claim_if_failed: The candidate's stability mechanism is not established; preserve all diagnostics and do not lower the floor after observing outcomes.

forecast_gate.gate_failure_action: Continue only to labeled descriptive consensus-policy diagnostics; deny AI action review, promotion, leverage, and untouched data.

descriptive_policy_contract.position_sleeves: Fixed one-third notional sleeve per symbol.
descriptive_policy_contract.entry_rule: When flat, evaluate every horizon. Long score is the minimum of the three seed median forecasts minus 12 bps round-trip cost. Short score is the negative maximum seed median forecast minus 12 bps. Enter only the side and horizon with the largest strictly positive score.
descriptive_policy_contract.rationale: The identical Round 45 mapping isolates the training mechanism and preserves an explicit epistemic-disagreement veto; changing the policy after seeing Round 45 outcomes is forbidden in this round.
descriptive_policy_contract.holding_rule: Hold for exactly the selected 1, 4, 12, or 24-hour horizon, then flatten. No overlapping position is permitted within a symbol.
descriptive_policy_contract.stress_rule: Reprice the exact fixed base trade ledger; do not reselect horizons, sides, or timestamps under stress.

economic_gate.promotion_result: Still denied because the complete Round 46 evaluation is selection-contaminated development evidence.

ai_contract.reason: The current AI benchmark establishes schema-constrained safety reasoning only. A paired AI-versus-deterministic after-cost uplift test remains forbidden until a deterministic candidate passes its numerical forecast, mechanism, and economic gates.

required_outputs: DirectML WaveBound and mutual-consistency backward-update preflight and runtime evidence
per-candidate per-seed artifacts and exact reload checks
hashed seed and ensemble prediction tensors
per-epoch supervised, WaveBound, EMA, and peer-consistency training diagnostics
per-candidate per-symbol per-horizon per-month forecast diagnostics
per-candidate seed stability and mechanism gate
per-candidate descriptive fixed consensus trade ledger
matched fixed-ledger base and stress economics
forecast, mechanism, and economic gate reasons
current hash-bound AI safety benchmark reference and explicit no-uplift boundary
peak working set runtime and progress events
